title: Bathroom Remodel Cost Calculator
subtitle: 7 questions, real numbers from Bozeman bathroom projects. Not national averages — actual Gallatin Valley material and labor costs.

steps.question: What kind of project is this?
steps.subtitle: This sets the baseline for everything else.
steps.why: A gut remodel means all-new plumbing, waterproofing, and subfloor. A refresh keeps most of that in place. This one question changes the estimate by thousands.

steps.question: How big is the bathroom?
steps.subtitle: A rough estimate is fine — we measure on-site.
steps.why: Tile, waterproofing, and heated floors are priced per square foot. Even a rough size meaningfully changes the range.

options.label: Small
options.desc: Under 40 sq ft · half bath / powder room

steps.question: What's your shower and tub setup?
steps.subtitle: What you want in the finished bathroom.
steps.why: Separate tub and shower means two drain locations, more tile, and more waterproofing — that's where the cost jumps. The rough-in alone can add $2,000+.

options.label: Separate tub & shower
options.desc: Freestanding tub plus a shower stall

options.label: Soaking tub only
options.desc: Soaking tub, no shower in this room

steps.question: What level of tile work?
steps.subtitle: Tile is usually the biggest design decision — and the biggest cost driver.
steps.why: Basic subway tile runs $3–5/sq ft installed. Natural stone and complex mosaics hit $25+/sq ft and take 3× longer. Tile choice alone can swing a bathroom by $5,000+.

options.label: Designed & detailed
options.desc: Mixed patterns, accent niche, quality tile

steps.question: What about fixtures and finishes?
steps.subtitle: Faucets, showerheads, hardware — the things you touch every day.
steps.why: A standard Moen faucet is $150. A Brizo or Kohler Purist is $400–600. Multiply that across every fixture in the room and the tier choice adds $2,000–$8,000.

steps.question: Any special features?
steps.subtitle: Select all that apply — or skip if none.
steps.why: Each of these is a standalone scope item with its own materials and labor. Heated floors alone need an electrician, dedicated circuit, and Ditra membrane — $1,200–$1,800.

steps.question: When are you hoping to start?
steps.subtitle: No pressure — this helps us plan our schedule.
steps.why: Bozeman contractors book up fast. Planning 3+ months out gives time to order specialty tile and materials at better pricing.

project_type.full_gut: A full gut means demo to studs — we inspect every layer and rebuild with modern waterproofing.
project_type.refresh: A refresh keeps the layout and plumbing positions but replaces all visible surfaces.
project_type.tub_to_shower: Tub-to-shower conversions involve plumbing relocation and new waterproofing at the drain.
project_type.specific_fix: Targeted repairs address the problem without unnecessary demo — most cost-effective approach.

bathroom_size.small: Small bathrooms are quicker builds but require careful layout planning — every inch matters.
bathroom_size.medium: Standard full bathrooms are our most common project — well-understood scope and timeline.
bathroom_size.large: Primary bathrooms have more tile area, often dual vanities, and larger shower builds.
bathroom_size.xl: Luxury primary suites involve complex tile patterns, freestanding tubs, and custom cabinetry.

shower_tub.shower_only: Walk-in showers are the most popular choice in Bozeman remodels right now.
shower_tub.tub_shower: Tub/shower combos are practical and cost-effective — still the standard in most homes.
shower_tub.both_separate: Separate tub and shower is a premium layout that adds significant plumbing and tile work.
shower_tub.tub_only: Soaking tubs are a design statement — we handle the structural support and drain positioning.

tile_level.basic: Clean subway or large-format tile installs quickly and looks timeless — great value.
tile_level.mid: Mixed patterns and accent features require more layout time but create a custom look.
tile_level.high: Natural stone and mosaic work is labor-intensive — this is where craftsmanship shows most.

fixtures.standard: Moen and Delta deliver reliable quality at a good price point — we install these regularly.
fixtures.upgraded: Kohler and Brizo fixtures are a noticeable step up in feel and finish.
fixtures.premium: High-end fixtures and custom vanities are the details that make a bathroom feel luxurious.

features.heated_floor: Electric radiant heat under tile — a game-changer for Bozeman's cold mornings.
features.niches: Built-in shower niches are tiled into the wall during the waterproofing stage.
features.bench: A tiled shower bench adds comfort and accessibility — built on a waterproofed frame.
features.double_vanity: Double vanities require wider plumbing rough-in and a larger countertop.
features.lighting: Updated lighting and wiring modernizes the space — we coordinate with a licensed electrician.
features.ventilation: Proper ventilation prevents moisture damage — critical in Montana's cold, dry winters.
